info: Upload this entire file to Firestore collection named: drugs. Each drug document ID = drug['id'] field. Delete JSON from app after uploading.
howToUpload: In Claude Code run: Read CLAUDE.md. Then create a one-time upload script that reads this JSON file from assets/drugs/drugs_firestore.json and uploads each drug as a Firestore document in collection 'drugs' with document ID = drug id field. Run once then delete script.

drugs.u: Paracetamol relieves mild to moderate pain and reduces fever. It is one of the safest and most widely used medicines worldwide, suitable for adults, children, and pregnant women. It works by blocking pain signals in the brain and reducing prostaglandins that cause fever.
drugs.s: Headache, Migraine, Toothache, Earache, Sore throat, Body aches, Muscle pain, Joint pain, Backache, Period pain, Fever, Cold and flu symptoms, Post-vaccination fever, Pain after surgery
drugs.d: Adults: 500mg to 1000mg every 4-6 hours. Maximum 4000mg per day. Children: 10-15mg per kg every 4-6 hours. Do not exceed 4 doses in 24 hours.
drugs.se: Very safe at normal doses. Rare: skin rash, allergic reaction. Overdose causes serious liver damage even without initial symptoms.
drugs.w: Never exceed recommended dose. Avoid alcohol. Do not combine with other paracetamol-containing medicines. Overdose requires immediate emergency treatment.

drugs.u: Ibuprofen is a non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) that relieves pain, reduces inflammation, and lowers fever. It works by blocking COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes that produce prostaglandins responsible for pain and inflammation. It is effective for a wide range of painful conditions.
drugs.s: Headache, Dental pain, Menstrual cramps, Muscle pain, Joint pain, Arthritis, Sports injuries, Back pain, Fever, Cold symptoms, Post-surgical pain, Inflammation, Sprains
drugs.d: Adults: 200-400mg every 4-6 hours. Maximum 1200mg daily without prescription. With doctor advice up to 3200mg daily. Always take with food or milk. Children: 5-10mg per kg every 6-8 hours.
drugs.se: Common: stomach upset, heartburn, nausea, dizziness. Serious: stomach ulcers and bleeding, kidney problems, increased blood pressure, fluid retention, allergic reactions.
drugs.w: Always take with food. Avoid in pregnancy especially last 3 months. Use caution in elderly and patients with kidney, liver or heart disease. Use lowest effective dose.

drugs.u: Amoxicillin is a broad-spectrum penicillin antibiotic used to treat a wide range of bacterial infections. It works by preventing bacteria from building their cell walls causing them to die. It is one of the most commonly prescribed antibiotics for children and adults.
drugs.s: Ear infections, Throat infections and tonsillitis, Chest infections and pneumonia, Urinary tract infections, Skin infections, Dental abscesses, Sinusitis, H. pylori stomach infection, Lyme disease, Typhoid fever
drugs.d: Adults: 250-500mg three times daily. Severe infections: 500-1000mg three times daily. Children: 25-45mg per kg per day in divided doses. Always complete the full course even if feeling better.
drugs.se: Common: diarrhea, stomach upset, nausea, vomiting, skin rash. Serious: severe allergic reaction (anaphylaxis), severe skin rash, liver problems.
drugs.w: Tell doctor if allergic to penicillin. Complete full antibiotic course. Do not use for viral infections like cold or flu.

drugs.u: Augmentin is a combination antibiotic containing amoxicillin and clavulanate potassium. Clavulanate helps amoxicillin work against resistant bacteria. It is stronger than plain amoxicillin and commonly known as Calamox in Pakistan. Used for more serious or resistant bacterial infections.
drugs.s: Severe ear infections, Sinusitis, Chest infections and pneumonia, Skin and soft tissue infections, Urinary tract infections, Dental infections, Animal bite infections, Bone and joint infections, Resistant bacterial infections
drugs.d: Adults: 375mg to 625mg two to three times daily with food. Severe infections: 1000mg twice daily. Children: dose based on weight. Always take with food to reduce stomach upset.
drugs.se: Common: diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, skin rash, stomach pain. Serious: liver damage, severe allergic reaction, severe diarrhea from C. difficile.
drugs.w: Always take with food. Tell doctor about penicillin allergy. Complete full course. More likely to cause diarrhea than plain amoxicillin.

drugs.u: Azithromycin is a macrolide antibiotic widely used for respiratory and skin infections. It accumulates in infected tissues allowing short 3-5 day treatment courses. It remains active in the body for up to 10 days after the last dose continuing to fight infection.
drugs.s: Chest infections and pneumonia, Throat infections, Ear infections, Skin infections, Sexually transmitted infections including chlamydia, Sinusitis, Typhoid fever, H. pylori infection, Traveler's diarrhea
drugs.d: Adults: 500mg once daily for 3 days for most infections. Or 500mg on day 1 then 250mg daily for 4 more days. Children: 10mg per kg once daily for 3 days. Can be taken with or without food.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, diarrhea, abdominal pain, vomiting, headache. Less common: heart rhythm problems, liver enzyme elevation, dizziness.
drugs.w: Tell doctor about heart problems. Avoid antacids within 2 hours of dose. Use with caution in liver disease. May interact with warfarin.

drugs.u: Ciprofloxacin is a fluoroquinolone antibiotic with broad-spectrum activity. It is particularly effective for urinary tract infections, typhoid fever (common in Pakistan), and respiratory infections. It works by preventing bacteria from copying their DNA.
drugs.s: Urinary tract infections and kidney infections, Typhoid fever, Traveler's diarrhea, Respiratory tract infections, Skin and soft tissue infections, Bone and joint infections, Sexually transmitted infections, Eye infections, Prostatitis
drugs.d: Adults: 250-750mg twice daily depending on infection type. UTI: 250mg twice daily for 3-7 days. Typhoid: 500mg twice daily for 10-14 days. Take on empty stomach or with light meal.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, diarrhea, headache, dizziness, abdominal pain. Serious: tendon rupture especially Achilles tendon, peripheral neuropathy, mental health effects, heart rhythm problems.
drugs.w: Avoid in children under 18 except specific indications. Avoid in pregnancy. Stop immediately if tendon pain occurs. Do not take with antacids or calcium within 2-4 hours.

drugs.u: Metronidazole is an antibiotic and antiprotozoal highly effective against anaerobic bacteria and parasites. Very commonly used in Pakistan for gastrointestinal infections, dental infections, vaginal infections, and protozoal diseases including amoebiasis and giardiasis.
drugs.s: Dental infections and abscesses, Stomach and intestinal infections, Vaginal infections including bacterial vaginosis and trichomoniasis, Amoebiasis, Giardiasis, Liver abscess, Skin infections, H. pylori eradication, Pelvic inflammatory disease
drugs.d: Adults: 400mg three times daily for 5-7 days. Amoebiasis: 800mg three times daily for 5-10 days. Dental infections: 200-400mg three times daily for 3-7 days. Children: 7.5mg per kg three times daily. Take with food.
drugs.se: Common: metallic taste in mouth (very common), nausea, headache, diarrhea, stomach cramps, dark urine (harmless). Serious: nerve damage with prolonged use, seizures at high doses.
drugs.w: STRICTLY avoid all alcohol during treatment AND for 48 hours after completing course. Causes severe reaction with alcohol. Avoid in first trimester of pregnancy. Can interact with warfarin.

drugs.u: Omeprazole is a proton pump inhibitor (PPI) that dramatically reduces stomach acid production by blocking the acid pump. It is one of the most commonly prescribed medicines worldwide and is sold as Risek in Pakistan. Very effective for acid reflux, ulcers, and H. pylori infection.
drugs.s: Acid reflux and heartburn, Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), Stomach ulcers, Duodenal ulcers, H. pylori infection (combined with antibiotics), Zollinger-Ellison syndrome, NSAID-induced stomach damage, Esophagitis, Upper stomach pain
drugs.d: Adults: 20mg once daily before breakfast for most conditions. Ulcers: 20-40mg once daily for 4-8 weeks. H. pylori: 20mg twice daily with antibiotics for 7-14 days. Take 30-60 minutes before eating.
drugs.se: Common: headache, diarrhea, nausea, stomach pain, constipation. Long-term: low magnesium, vitamin B12 deficiency, bone fracture risk, kidney problems, increased infection risk.
drugs.w: Take 30 minutes before eating. Do not use long term without medical supervision. May mask symptoms of serious conditions. Regular monitoring needed for long-term use.

drugs.u: Pantoprazole is a proton pump inhibitor with fewer drug interactions than omeprazole, making it preferred when patients take multiple medicines. It provides sustained reduction of gastric acid and is available in oral and injectable forms for hospital use.
drugs.s: Acid reflux and heartburn, GERD, Gastric and duodenal ulcers, H. pylori eradication, Zollinger-Ellison syndrome, Prevention of NSAID-induced ulcers, Erosive esophagitis, Upper abdominal pain
drugs.d: Adults: 40mg once daily 30-60 minutes before breakfast. Severe cases: 40mg twice daily. H. pylori: 40mg twice daily with antibiotics for 7-14 days. Take before meals for best effect.
drugs.se: Common: headache, diarrhea, nausea, abdominal pain. Long-term: low magnesium, vitamin B12 deficiency, bone fracture risk.
drugs.w: Do not crush or chew tablets. Take before eating. Avoid long-term use without medical advice. Fewer drug interactions than omeprazole.

drugs.u: Metformin is the first-line medicine for type 2 diabetes and one of the most prescribed medicines globally. It reduces glucose production in the liver, improves insulin sensitivity, and does not cause weight gain. It has additional benefits in reducing cardiovascular risk.
drugs.s: Type 2 diabetes mellitus, High blood sugar levels, Insulin resistance, Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), Prediabetes prevention of progression, Metabolic syndrome
drugs.d: Adults: Start 500mg once or twice daily with meals. Increase to 1500-2000mg daily. Maximum 3000mg per day. Always take with or immediately after meals to minimize stomach upset.
drugs.se: Very common initially: nausea, diarrhea, stomach upset, metallic taste. Usually improve within 2-4 weeks. Rare but serious: lactic acidosis (especially in kidney or liver disease).
drugs.w: Stop before contrast dye procedures and surgery. Avoid excessive alcohol. Check kidney function regularly. Do not use if kidney function is significantly impaired.

drugs.u: Amlodipine is a calcium channel blocker that relaxes blood vessel walls allowing blood to flow more easily. It provides smooth 24-hour blood pressure control from a single daily dose and is among the most prescribed blood pressure medicines worldwide.
drugs.s: Hypertension (high blood pressure), Stable angina (chest pain), Coronary artery disease, Prevention of cardiovascular events, Raynaud's phenomenon
drugs.d: Adults: Start with 5mg once daily. Can increase to 10mg once daily after 1-2 weeks if needed. Elderly: start with 2.5mg. Take at same time each day with or without food.
drugs.se: Common: ankle and foot swelling (up to 10% of patients), flushing, headache, dizziness, palpitations. Usually improve after a few weeks.
drugs.w: Do not stop suddenly without doctor advice. Grapefruit juice may increase drug levels. Regular blood pressure monitoring essential.

drugs.u: Atorvastatin is the most potent statin used to lower LDL cholesterol and reduce cardiovascular risk. It works by inhibiting HMG-CoA reductase in the liver. Studies show it significantly reduces heart attacks, strokes, and cardiovascular death. Usually taken as a lifelong medicine.
drugs.s: High LDL cholesterol, High triglycerides, Prevention of heart attack and stroke, Established cardiovascular disease, Familial hypercholesterolemia, Diabetes with cardiovascular risk factors
drugs.d: Adults: Usually 10-20mg once daily. Can increase to 40-80mg daily. Take at any time of day with or without food. Consistency is more important than timing.
drugs.se: Common: muscle aches and pain, headache, nausea, diarrhea, joint pain. Serious: severe muscle breakdown (rhabdomyolysis), liver damage.
drugs.w: Report unexplained muscle pain immediately. Avoid grapefruit juice. Regular liver function tests. Not safe in pregnancy or breastfeeding.

drugs.u: Losartan is an angiotensin receptor blocker (ARB) that blocks angiotensin II causing blood vessels to relax. Unlike ACE inhibitors it does not cause dry cough. It has specific benefits in protecting kidneys in diabetic patients and reducing stroke risk.
drugs.s: Hypertension, Type 2 diabetic nephropathy (kidney protection), Heart failure, Stroke prevention, Reduction of cardiovascular events
drugs.d: Adults: Usually 50mg once daily. Increase to 100mg once daily if needed. Elderly: start with 25mg. Take with or without food at same time each day.
drugs.se: Common: dizziness, upper respiratory infections, back pain, high potassium levels, low blood pressure. Rare: kidney problems, angioedema.
drugs.w: Not safe in pregnancy. Monitor potassium and kidney function regularly. Avoid potassium supplements unless prescribed. Drink adequate fluids.

drugs.u: Aspirin has two main uses. At higher doses it relieves pain, fever, and inflammation. At low doses (75-100mg) it prevents blood clots, heart attacks, and strokes. It irreversibly inhibits platelet aggregation making it invaluable for cardiovascular prevention.
drugs.s: Pain relief (higher doses): Headache, Fever, Muscle pain, Toothache, Period pain, Joint inflammation. Cardiovascular prevention (low doses): Heart attack prevention, Stroke prevention, After coronary stent placement, Angina
drugs.d: Pain and fever adults: 300-900mg every 4-6 hours. Maximum 4000mg per day. Cardiovascular prevention: 75-100mg once daily with food. Never give to children under 16.
drugs.se: Common: stomach irritation, heartburn, nausea, bleeding tendency. Serious: stomach ulcers and bleeding, allergic reactions, tinnitus at high doses, Reye syndrome in children.
drugs.w: Never give to children under 16 years. Avoid in last 3 months of pregnancy. Increases bleeding risk. Take with food. Stop before surgery.

drugs.u: Diclofenac is one of the most potent NSAIDs widely used for pain and inflammation. Available in tablets, injections, gels, and eye drops. It selectively inhibits COX-2 more than COX-1 potentially causing less stomach irritation than older NSAIDs.
drugs.s: Rheumatoid arthritis, Osteoarthritis, Ankylosing spondylitis, Acute gout, Sports injuries, Back pain, Dental pain, Post-surgical pain, Menstrual cramps, Migraine, Eye inflammation
drugs.d: Adults: 75-150mg daily in divided doses. Modified-release: 75-100mg once or twice daily. Gel: apply 2-4g to affected area 3-4 times daily. Always take oral forms with food.
drugs.se: Common: stomach upset, nausea, heartburn, diarrhea, headache. Serious: stomach ulcers, kidney damage, liver damage, cardiovascular events, high blood pressure.
drugs.w: Higher cardiovascular risk than some other NSAIDs. Avoid in pregnancy especially last 3 months. Use lowest effective dose for shortest time.

drugs.u: Cetirizine is a second-generation antihistamine that blocks H1 histamine receptors with minimal sedation. It provides 24-hour relief from allergy symptoms with once-daily dosing and is safe for adults and children.
drugs.s: Seasonal allergic rhinitis (hay fever), Perennial allergic rhinitis, Allergic conjunctivitis, Chronic urticaria (hives), Itchy skin rashes, Insect bite reactions, Pet and animal allergies, Dust mite allergy
drugs.d: Adults and children over 12: 10mg once daily. Children 6-12 years: 5mg twice daily or 10mg once daily. Children 2-6 years: 2.5mg twice daily. Take with or without food.
drugs.se: Usually well tolerated. Common: mild drowsiness, dry mouth, headache, dizziness, fatigue, nausea.
drugs.w: May cause some drowsiness. Use caution when driving. Avoid alcohol. Reduce dose in kidney disease.

drugs.u: Loratadine is a non-drowsy antihistamine that provides 24-hour allergy relief. It works by blocking histamine without significantly crossing the blood-brain barrier making it suitable for daytime use.
drugs.s: Hay fever symptoms, Sneezing and runny nose, Itchy and watery eyes, Skin hives and itching, Year-round allergic rhinitis, Pet dander allergy, Dust mite allergy, Pollen allergy
drugs.d: Adults and children over 12: 10mg once daily. Children 2-12 years: 5mg if under 30kg, 10mg if over 30kg. Take with or without food.
drugs.se: Very few side effects. Occasionally: headache, dry mouth, fatigue. Rarely causes drowsiness.
drugs.w: One of the safest antihistamines. Suitable for daytime use. Reduce dose in kidney or liver disease.

drugs.u: Salbutamol is a short-acting bronchodilator (reliever inhaler) that rapidly relaxes airway muscles allowing easier breathing within 5 minutes. It is the most commonly prescribed reliever inhaler for asthma and should always be carried by asthma patients.
drugs.s: Acute asthma attacks, Breathing difficulty and wheezing, Chest tightness in asthma, COPD acute exacerbations, Exercise-induced bronchospasm prevention, Allergic bronchospasm, Acute bronchitis with wheeze
drugs.d: Relief of acute attack: 1-2 puffs (100-200 micrograms) as needed. Allow 30-60 seconds between puffs. Exercise prevention: 2 puffs 15-30 minutes before. Maximum 8-10 puffs in 24 hours.
drugs.se: Common: fine tremor of hands, headache, increased heart rate, nervousness, muscle cramps, low potassium at high doses.
drugs.w: This is a reliever not a preventer. If using more than 3 times per week seek medical review. Always carry your reliever inhaler. Shake inhaler before use.

drugs.u: Sertraline is an SSRI antidepressant that increases serotonin levels in the brain helping regulate mood, sleep, and anxiety. It is one of the most widely prescribed antidepressants due to its effectiveness and tolerability. Requires 4-6 weeks for full therapeutic effect.
drugs.s: Major depressive disorder, Generalized anxiety disorder (GAD), Panic disorder, Social anxiety disorder, Obsessive compulsive disorder (OCD), Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), Premenstrual dysphoric disorder (PMDD)
drugs.d: Adults: Start 50mg once daily. Increase to 100-200mg after 1-2 weeks if needed. OCD: up to 200mg daily. Take at same time each day with or without food.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, diarrhea, insomnia, dry mouth, sweating, sexual dysfunction. Usually improve after first 2-4 weeks.
drugs.w: Do not stop suddenly. Monitor young adults for suicidal thoughts. Takes 4-6 weeks for full effect. Avoid alcohol. Do not take with MAO inhibitors.

drugs.u: Warfarin is a blood thinner that prevents blood clots by blocking vitamin K-dependent clotting factors. The dose must be carefully monitored with regular INR blood tests. Despite complexity it remains widely used because antidotes are available.
drugs.s: Prevention of deep vein thrombosis (DVT), Pulmonary embolism treatment, Stroke prevention in atrial fibrillation, Blood clot prevention after mechanical heart valve replacement, Prevention of recurrent heart attack
drugs.d: Dose is highly individualized based on regular INR blood testing. Typical starting dose 5mg daily. Adjust to maintain INR between 2.0-3.0 for most conditions. Small dose changes significantly affect INR.
drugs.se: Main risk: bleeding at any site including nosebleeds, gum bleeding, heavy periods, bruising, blood in urine or stools. Serious: intracranial bleeding, major gastrointestinal bleeding.
drugs.w: Regular INR testing essential. Many food interactions especially vitamin K foods (spinach, kale, broccoli). Many medicine interactions. Tell every doctor and dentist. Wear medical alert bracelet. Report unusual bleeding immediately.

drugs.u: Clopidogrel prevents blood clots by irreversibly blocking platelet aggregation. Commonly used after heart attack, stroke, or coronary stents to prevent re-thrombosis. Often combined with aspirin for dual antiplatelet therapy.
drugs.s: Prevention of recurrent heart attack, Prevention of recurrent ischemic stroke, After coronary stent placement (critical), Acute coronary syndrome, Peripheral arterial disease, Prevention of cardiovascular events in high-risk patients
drugs.d: Adults: 75mg once daily with or without food. Loading dose after acute coronary syndrome or stent: 300-600mg as single dose, then 75mg daily.
drugs.se: Common: bruising, bleeding from cuts, nosebleeds, stomach pain, nausea, diarrhea. Serious: severe bleeding, thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (rare).
drugs.w: NEVER stop without consulting cardiologist especially after stent as this can cause fatal stent thrombosis. Increased bleeding risk during surgery. Tell all doctors.

drugs.u: Furosemide is a potent loop diuretic that dramatically increases urine output by preventing sodium and water reabsorption in the kidneys. It works within 30-60 minutes. Essential treatment for heart failure, pulmonary edema, and severe fluid retention.
drugs.s: Congestive heart failure with fluid retention, Pulmonary edema (acute fluid in lungs - emergency), Edema from kidney disease, Edema from liver cirrhosis, Hypertension, Severe fluid overload
drugs.d: Adults: 20-80mg once daily in the morning. Maximum 600mg daily in severe cases. IV for acute pulmonary edema: 40-80mg by slow injection. Take in morning to avoid nighttime urination.
drugs.se: Very common: increased urination, dehydration, low potassium (muscle cramps, weakness), low sodium, dizziness on standing. Less common: hearing problems at high doses, gout.
drugs.w: Take in the morning. Monitor electrolytes especially potassium regularly. Potassium supplements often needed. Avoid excessive sun. Stand up slowly to prevent dizziness.

drugs.u: Levothyroxine is a synthetic thyroid hormone (T4) used to replace inadequate thyroid hormone in hypothyroidism. In the body it converts to the active T3 hormone. Most patients need to continue this medicine for life. Regular TSH blood tests are needed to adjust the dose.
drugs.s: Hypothyroidism (underactive thyroid gland), Hashimoto's thyroiditis, Thyroid hormone deficiency, Goiter prevention and treatment, After thyroid surgery or radioiodine treatment, Thyroid cancer suppression
drugs.d: Adults: Start 25-50 micrograms daily. Increase by 25-50mcg every 4-6 weeks based on TSH blood test results. Usual maintenance 100-200 micrograms daily. Take on empty stomach 30-60 minutes before breakfast.
drugs.se: When correctly dosed: no side effects. When dose too high: rapid heartbeat, tremor, anxiety, insomnia, weight loss, excessive sweating. When too low: persistent hypothyroid symptoms.
drugs.w: Take on empty stomach 30-60 minutes before breakfast with water only. Calcium, iron tablets, antacids reduce absorption. Regular TSH blood tests essential to adjust dose.

drugs.u: Allopurinol reduces uric acid production by inhibiting xanthine oxidase enzyme. It prevents future gout attacks and dissolves existing tophi over time. Also prevents kidney stones caused by uric acid. Takes several months before full benefit is seen.
drugs.s: Prevention of recurrent gout attacks, Lowering elevated uric acid levels (hyperuricemia), Prevention of uric acid kidney stones, Chronic tophaceous gout, Tumor lysis syndrome prevention in cancer treatment
drugs.d: Adults: Start 100mg once daily after meals. Increase by 100mg every 2-4 weeks. Target uric acid below 360 micromol per liter. Usual maintenance 200-300mg daily. Up to 600-900mg for severe cases.
drugs.se: Common: skin rash (must stop immediately if develops), nausea, diarrhea, drowsiness. Serious: Stevens-Johnson syndrome, liver toxicity, bone marrow suppression.
drugs.w: IMMEDIATELY stop if skin rash develops and seek urgent medical attention. Do not start during an acute gout attack. Do not start until 2-4 weeks after an acute attack. Drink 2+ liters water daily.

drugs.u: Prednisolone is a powerful corticosteroid with anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive effects. It suppresses the immune system and reduces inflammation throughout the body. Effective for a vast range of inflammatory and autoimmune conditions but has significant side effects with prolonged use.
drugs.s: Severe asthma attacks, Rheumatoid arthritis flares, Inflammatory bowel disease, Systemic lupus erythematosus, Severe allergic reactions, Eczema and psoriasis flares, Kidney disease, Blood disorders, Eye inflammation, Transplant rejection prevention
drugs.d: Acute conditions adults: 20-60mg daily in divided doses. Maintenance usually 5-15mg daily. Asthma attack: 30-40mg once daily for 5 days. Always take with food. Taper dose gradually after prolonged treatment.
drugs.se: Short-term: increased appetite, weight gain, mood changes, insomnia, raised blood sugar. Long-term: osteoporosis, muscle wasting, thin skin, cataracts, increased infection risk, adrenal suppression, hypertension.
drugs.w: NEVER stop suddenly after prolonged use as this can cause adrenal crisis. Carry steroid card. Increased risk of serious infections. Monitor blood sugar in diabetics. Avoid live vaccines.

drugs.u: Domperidone is a dopamine antagonist that relieves nausea and vomiting and speeds up stomach emptying. Unlike metoclopramide it does not significantly cross the blood-brain barrier making it safer. It does not cause the movement disorders seen with metoclopramide.
drugs.s: Nausea and vomiting from various causes, Delayed gastric emptying (gastroparesis), Bloating and fullness after eating, Acid reflux with slow stomach emptying, Nausea from Parkinson's medications, Functional dyspepsia
drugs.d: Adults: 10mg up to three times daily 15-30 minutes before meals. Maximum 30mg per day. Use lowest effective dose for shortest period.
drugs.se: Common: dry mouth, headache, diarrhea, galactorrhea (abnormal milk production), menstrual irregularities. Rare but serious: cardiac arrhythmias especially at high doses in elderly.
drugs.w: Use lowest dose for shortest time. Risk of cardiac arrhythmias at higher doses especially in elderly. Avoid in patients with cardiac conditions or liver disease. Do not exceed 30mg daily.

drugs.u: Ondansetron is a selective serotonin receptor antagonist that prevents nausea and vomiting by blocking serotonin receptors in the gut and brain. It is the most effective antiemetic for chemotherapy-induced nausea and is standard of care for highly emetogenic chemotherapy regimens.
drugs.s: Nausea and vomiting from chemotherapy, Nausea from radiotherapy, Post-operative nausea and vomiting, Severe morning sickness in pregnancy, Acute gastroenteritis vomiting, Migraine with severe nausea
drugs.d: Chemotherapy adults: 8mg by slow IV before chemotherapy then 8mg every 8-12 hours for 1-5 days. Post-operative: 4mg IV at induction. Oral for general nausea: 4-8mg up to three times daily.
drugs.se: Common: headache, constipation, flushing, sensation of warmth. Serious: QT interval prolongation, serotonin syndrome.
drugs.w: ECG monitoring recommended in heart conditions. Can cause constipation. Serotonin syndrome risk with other serotonergic medicines.

drugs.u: Loperamide acts on opioid receptors in the gut wall reducing intestinal motility and secretion. It provides rapid symptom relief from diarrhea. It does not cross the blood-brain barrier so has no addictive properties. Always use with ORS to prevent dehydration.
drugs.s: Acute diarrhea including traveler's diarrhea, Gastroenteritis, Chronic diarrhea (IBS-D), Diarrhea after intestinal surgery, Chemotherapy-induced diarrhea
drugs.d: Adults: 4mg initially then 2mg after each loose stool. Maximum 16mg per day. Children (over 8 years): 2mg after each loose stool. Always drink plenty of fluids. Seek medical advice if no improvement after 48 hours.
drugs.se: Common: constipation, stomach cramps, bloating, nausea, dizziness. Serious with prolonged use: toxic megacolon, ileus.
drugs.w: Do not use if bloody or mucus diarrhea. Stop if no improvement within 48 hours. Not suitable for children under 2 years. Always rehydrate well.

drugs.u: Folic acid (vitamin B9) is essential for DNA synthesis and cell division. It is critically important in early pregnancy as it prevents neural tube defects such as spina bifida and anencephaly. All women who might become pregnant should take folic acid daily as defects develop in first 28 days, often before pregnancy is known.
drugs.s: Prevention of neural tube defects in pregnancy (spina bifida, anencephaly), Folate deficiency anemia, Megaloblastic anemia, Prevention of folate deficiency in patients on methotrexate or phenytoin, Pre-conception supplementation
drugs.d: Prevention of neural tube defects: 400 micrograms daily starting at least one month before conception through first 12 weeks. Women with diabetes, epilepsy, or previous neural tube defect-affected pregnancy: 5mg daily. Deficiency treatment: 5mg once daily for 4 months.
drugs.se: Extremely safe. Very rare allergic reactions. High doses may mask vitamin B12 deficiency.
drugs.w: Start at least one month before trying to conceive. All women of childbearing age should consider daily supplementation. Women with epilepsy or diabetes need higher 5mg dose. Consult doctor about appropriate dose.

drugs.u: Vitamin D3 is essential for calcium absorption, bone health, immune function, and muscle function. Deficiency is extremely prevalent in Pakistan and South Asia due to cultural practices of skin covering and limited outdoor activity. Severe deficiency causes rickets in children and osteomalacia in adults.
drugs.s: Vitamin D deficiency and insufficiency, Rickets in children, Osteomalacia in adults, Osteoporosis prevention, Prevention of falls in elderly, Immune system support, Seasonal affective disorder linked to low vitamin D
drugs.d: Deficiency treatment: 1000-4000 IU daily for 3-6 months. Severe deficiency: 50000 IU weekly for 8-12 weeks under supervision. Prevention: 600-2000 IU daily. Infants: 400 IU daily.
drugs.se: At recommended doses: very safe. Toxicity from excessive doses: nausea, vomiting, weakness, high calcium levels, kidney stones. Toxicity is rare with doses under 10000 IU daily.
drugs.w: Blood test before and during treatment to guide dosing. Do not exceed recommended doses without blood monitoring. Calcium supplementation often recommended alongside.

drugs.u: Ferrous sulphate provides elemental iron for treating iron deficiency anemia. Iron deficiency is the most common nutritional deficiency worldwide, especially in women of reproductive age, children, and pregnant women. Causes fatigue, weakness, and reduced work capacity.
drugs.s: Iron deficiency anemia, Fatigue and weakness from low iron, Prevention of anemia in pregnancy, Anemia from heavy menstrual bleeding, Anemia from poor dietary intake, Prevention of anemia in premature babies
drugs.d: Adults: 200mg (65mg elemental iron) two to three times daily. Pregnant women: 200mg once or twice daily. Children: 3-6mg elemental iron per kg per day. Taking on empty stomach improves absorption.
drugs.se: Very common: nausea, constipation, stomach cramps, diarrhea, black stools (completely harmless and expected). Significant digestive upset in some patients.
drugs.w: Black or dark stools are expected and harmless. Vitamin C improves absorption. Avoid taking with tea, coffee, dairy, antacids, or calcium supplements as these reduce absorption. Keep out of reach of children.

drugs.u: Co-trimoxazole (Septran, Bactrim) is a synergistic combination that blocks two sequential steps in bacterial folate synthesis providing a bactericidal effect. Widely used in Pakistan for urinary tract infections and respiratory infections. Also the drug of choice for Pneumocystis pneumonia in HIV patients.
drugs.s: Urinary tract infections, Acute otitis media (ear infections), Community-acquired pneumonia, Traveler's diarrhea, Shigellosis, Typhoid fever, Pneumocystis pneumonia in HIV patients, Nocardiosis
drugs.d: Adults: 960mg (2 standard tablets or 1 double-strength tablet) twice daily for most infections. UTI: can use for 3-7 days. PCP prophylaxis in HIV: 480-960mg daily or three times weekly. Children: dose based on weight.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, vomiting, rash, diarrhea. Serious: severe skin reactions (Stevens-Johnson syndrome), blood disorders, kidney damage, photosensitivity, high potassium.
drugs.w: Tell doctor about sulfa drug allergy. Drink plenty of fluids. Avoid excessive sun. Monitor blood counts and kidney function with long-term use. Avoid in last month of pregnancy.

drugs.u: Clotrimazole is an imidazole antifungal effective against dermatophytes (causing athlete's foot, ringworm), Candida (causing thrush), and Malassezia. Available in cream, powder, spray, vaginal pessary, and lozenges for different types of fungal infections.
drugs.s: Athlete's foot (tinea pedis), Ringworm (tinea corporis), Jock itch (tinea cruris), Vaginal thrush (candidal vaginitis), Oral thrush (lozenges), Skin fold infections, Pityriasis versicolor, Fungal nail infections
drugs.d: Skin cream: apply twice daily for 2-4 weeks. Continue for 2 weeks after symptoms resolve. Vaginal pessary: single 500mg pessary, or 200mg for 3 nights, or 100mg for 6 nights.
drugs.se: Usually well tolerated. Common: mild burning, stinging, or irritation at application site. Rare: allergic contact dermatitis.
drugs.w: Avoid contact with eyes. Can weaken latex condoms and diaphragms. If symptoms persist or recur frequently consult doctor as may indicate diabetes.

drugs.u: Fluconazole is an oral antifungal achieving excellent systemic concentrations. A single 150mg dose for vaginal thrush has revolutionized treatment. Also used for serious systemic fungal infections in immunocompromised patients.
drugs.s: Vaginal thrush (single 150mg dose), Oral and esophageal thrush, Cryptococcal meningitis in HIV patients, Systemic Candida infections, Prevention of fungal infections in cancer patients, Ringworm, Nail fungal infections
drugs.d: Vaginal thrush: single 150mg oral dose. Oral thrush: 50-100mg daily for 7-14 days. Esophageal candidiasis: 200mg on day 1 then 100mg daily for 3 weeks. Cryptococcal meningitis: 400-800mg daily.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, abdominal pain, headache, skin rash, elevated liver enzymes. Serious: severe liver damage, severe skin reactions, heart rhythm problems.
drugs.w: Many important drug interactions. Tell doctor all medicines being taken. Liver function tests if long-term use. Avoid in pregnancy especially first trimester.

drugs.u: Hydroxychloroquine is used for both malaria and autoimmune conditions. In autoimmune diseases it modulates the immune system reducing inflammation. Benefits develop slowly over months. Eye toxicity is the main long-term concern requiring annual screening.
drugs.s: Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) first-line treatment, Rheumatoid arthritis, Discoid lupus, Malaria prevention and treatment, Sjogren's syndrome, Skin manifestations of lupus
drugs.d: Lupus and rheumatoid arthritis: 200-400mg daily, never exceeding 5mg per kg body weight per day. Malaria prevention: 400mg once weekly starting 2 weeks before travel.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, stomach cramps, diarrhea, headache, dizziness, skin rash. Serious and dose-dependent: retinopathy causing permanent vision loss with cumulative doses over many years.
drugs.w: Annual eye examination mandatory after 5 years. Baseline ophthalmology before starting. Take with food. Report any vision changes immediately.

drugs.u: Acyclovir is an antiviral selectively activated in herpes-infected cells. It does not cure herpes infections but significantly reduces symptoms, accelerates healing, and when taken prophylactically prevents recurrences. Start treatment at very first sign for maximum benefit.
drugs.s: Genital herpes (first episode and recurrences), Cold sores (cream applied early), Chickenpox especially in adults and immunocompromised, Shingles - reduces duration and post-herpetic neuralgia, Herpes encephalitis (brain infection - IV), Prevention of herpes in immunocompromised
drugs.d: Cold sores cream: apply 5 times daily at 4-hour intervals for 5 days. Genital herpes tablets: 200-400mg five times daily for 5-10 days. Shingles: 800mg five times daily for 7 days. Start as early as possible.
drugs.se: Topical cream: local stinging, burning. Oral tablets: nausea, headache, diarrhea, fatigue. IV high doses: kidney problems, neurological effects.
drugs.w: Start treatment at earliest sign for maximum benefit. Topical cream should not be used in mouth eyes or genitalia. Drink plenty of water with high oral doses.

drugs.u: Metoprolol is a cardioselective beta-1 blocker that reduces heart rate, blood pressure, and myocardial oxygen consumption. Its cardioselectivity makes it safer than non-selective beta-blockers in patients with mild asthma. Proven to reduce mortality after heart attack and in heart failure.
drugs.s: Hypertension, Angina, After heart attack (reduces mortality), Chronic heart failure, Atrial fibrillation rate control, Supraventricular tachycardia, Hyperthyroidism symptom control, Migraine prevention
drugs.d: Hypertension: 50-100mg once or twice daily (standard) or 50-200mg once daily (extended-release). Angina: 50-100mg two to three times daily. Post-MI: 100-200mg daily in divided doses.
drugs.se: Common: fatigue, bradycardia, cold extremities, dizziness, nausea, depression, sleep disturbances, impotence.
drugs.w: Never stop abruptly especially in angina. Taper over 1-2 weeks. Can worsen asthma. Masks hypoglycemia symptoms in diabetics. Monitor pulse rate regularly.

drugs.u: Ramipril is an ACE inhibitor that blocks the conversion of angiotensin I to II. The HOPE trial showed it reduces cardiovascular events in high-risk patients. It also has kidney protective effects in diabetic nephropathy. It causes the typical ACE inhibitor dry cough in 10-15% of patients.
drugs.s: Hypertension, Congestive heart failure, Prevention of cardiovascular events in high-risk patients, Diabetic nephropathy kidney protection, Post-myocardial infarction heart failure, Reduction of albuminuria in diabetes
drugs.d: Hypertension: Start 2.5mg once daily. Increase to 5-10mg once daily after 2-4 weeks. Heart failure: Start 1.25mg twice daily. Increase to 5mg twice daily over several weeks.
drugs.se: Common: dry persistent cough (up to 15%), dizziness, first-dose hypotension, elevated potassium, worsening kidney function. Rare but serious: angioedema (swelling of face, throat).
drugs.w: EMERGENCY: Stop immediately and call emergency services if face, throat, or tongue swelling occurs. Not safe in pregnancy. Monitor potassium and kidney function especially with diuretics.

drugs.u: Bisoprolol is a highly selective beta-1 blocker that is one of the few medicines proven to reduce mortality in chronic heart failure. Its high cardioselectivity minimizes airway effects. Dose must be started very low and titrated up slowly in heart failure.
drugs.s: Stable chronic heart failure (proven mortality benefit), Hypertension, Stable angina, Atrial fibrillation rate control, Heart rate reduction, Prevention of recurrent heart attack
drugs.d: Heart failure: Start very low at 1.25mg once daily. Double dose every 2 weeks as tolerated to target 10mg daily. This titration is critical. Hypertension and angina: 5-10mg once daily.
drugs.se: Common: fatigue, cold extremities, bradycardia (slow heart rate), sleep disturbances, dizziness, nausea, depression. Possible worsening of peripheral arterial disease.
drugs.w: Never stop suddenly especially in angina. Taper dose gradually over 1-2 weeks. Can worsen asthma though less than non-selective beta-blockers. Masks hypoglycemia in diabetics. Start at very low dose in heart failure.

drugs.u: Doxycycline is a second-generation tetracycline with improved absorption and longer half-life. It has broad spectrum activity including atypical bacteria. Particularly useful for respiratory infections, sexually transmitted diseases, malaria prevention, and acne.
drugs.s: Community-acquired pneumonia including atypical organisms, Sexually transmitted infections (chlamydia, gonorrhea), Malaria prevention in travelers, Lyme disease, Acne vulgaris, Rosacea, Rocky Mountain spotted fever, Anthrax, Brucellosis
drugs.d: Most infections: 100mg twice daily. Load with 200mg on first day. Malaria prevention: 100mg daily starting 1-2 days before travel, continuing during and 4 weeks after. Acne: 50-100mg daily. Take with full glass of water while sitting upright.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, sun sensitivity, esophageal irritation. In children: permanent tooth discoloration and bone development effects.
drugs.w: Avoid in pregnancy and children under 8 years. Take with full glass of water and remain upright for 30-60 minutes. Avoid dairy, antacids, and iron within 2 hours. Use sunscreen consistently.

drugs.u: Cefixime is a third-generation oral cephalosporin with excellent gram-negative activity. Commonly used in Pakistan for urinary tract infections, gonorrhea, respiratory infections, and typhoid fever. It achieves excellent urinary concentrations making it particularly effective for UTIs.
drugs.s: Urinary tract infections, Gonorrhea (uncomplicated), Community-acquired pneumonia, Acute otitis media (ear infections), Tonsillitis and pharyngitis, Typhoid fever, Enteric fever, Acute bronchitis exacerbation
drugs.d: Adults: 200-400mg daily as single dose or in two divided doses. Gonorrhea: 400mg as single dose. Children: 8mg per kg per day. Can be taken with or without food.
drugs.se: Common: diarrhea (most common), nausea, abdominal pain, skin rash. Serious: severe allergic reactions, C. difficile colitis, blood disorders.
drugs.w: Tell doctor about penicillin allergy due to potential cross-reactivity. Complete the full prescribed course. Report severe diarrhea during or after treatment.

drugs.u: Albendazole is a broad-spectrum anthelmintic that kills parasitic worms by inhibiting tubulin polymerization preventing glucose uptake. Highly effective against roundworms, tapeworms, and hookworms. Intestinal worm infections are extremely common in Pakistan particularly in children.
drugs.s: Roundworm infection (ascariasis), Hookworm infection, Whipworm infection, Pinworm infection, Tapeworm infections, Hydatid disease, Neurocysticercosis (tapeworm in brain), Giardiasis, Strongyloidiasis, Lymphatic filariasis
drugs.d: Intestinal worms adults and children over 2 years: 400mg as single dose. Can repeat after 3 weeks for pinworm. Hydatid disease: 400mg twice daily for 28-day cycles. Take with fatty food for systemic infections.
drugs.se: Single dose for intestinal worms: very well tolerated. Occasional nausea, headache, abdominal pain. With longer courses: liver enzyme elevation, hair loss, bone marrow suppression.
drugs.w: Single dose for intestinal worms is very safe. Avoid in first trimester of pregnancy. Liver function monitoring needed for prolonged courses. All family members should be treated for pinworm.

drugs.u: Gabapentin is used for epilepsy and neuropathic pain. It binds to voltage-gated calcium channels in the CNS reducing release of excitatory neurotransmitters. Particularly effective for diabetic neuropathy, post-herpetic neuralgia, and fibromyalgia.
drugs.s: Epilepsy (partial seizures), Diabetic peripheral neuropathy, Post-herpetic neuralgia (shingles pain), Fibromyalgia, Restless legs syndrome, Central neuropathic pain, Hot flushes after cancer treatment, Alcohol withdrawal
drugs.d: Epilepsy: 300mg on day 1, 300mg twice daily day 2, 300mg three times daily day 3. Increase to 900-3600mg daily in three divided doses. Neuropathic pain: similar titration. Dose must be reduced in kidney disease.
drugs.se: Very common: dizziness, drowsiness, unsteadiness, fatigue, weight gain, ankle swelling, blurred vision. Often dose-dependent.
drugs.w: Do not stop suddenly (seizure risk). Causes significant drowsiness so avoid driving initially. Risk of misuse. Reduce dose gradually over at least 1 week when stopping.

drugs.u: Pregabalin has the same mechanism as gabapentin but with more predictable absorption and greater potency. Approved for neuropathic pain, epilepsy, generalized anxiety disorder, and fibromyalgia. Significant abuse potential and controlled in many countries.
drugs.s: Diabetic peripheral neuropathy, Post-herpetic neuralgia, Fibromyalgia, Generalized anxiety disorder (GAD), Epilepsy adjunctive treatment, Spinal cord injury pain, Restless legs syndrome, Chronic back pain with nerve component
drugs.d: Neuropathic pain: Start 75mg twice daily. Increase to 300mg twice daily over 1-2 weeks. Anxiety: 75mg twice daily, may increase to 300mg twice daily. Maximum 600mg daily.
drugs.se: Very common: dizziness, drowsiness, significant weight gain, blurred vision, coordination problems, dry mouth, ankle swelling. More pronounced than gabapentin.
drugs.w: High potential for misuse and dependence. Do not stop suddenly. Significant driving impairment especially early. Avoid alcohol. Reduce dose in kidney disease. Weight gain can be substantial.

drugs.u: Esomeprazole is the S-isomer of omeprazole providing more consistent acid suppression. It is metabolized more slowly by the liver resulting in higher sustained blood levels. Commonly known as the purple pill and widely prescribed for acid-related conditions.
drugs.s: Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), Erosive esophagitis, Symptomatic GERD, H. pylori eradication, Prevention of NSAID-induced gastric ulcers, Zollinger-Ellison syndrome, Stomach ulcers, Heartburn
drugs.d: Adults: 20-40mg once daily 30-60 minutes before breakfast. Erosive esophagitis: 40mg once daily for 4-8 weeks. H. pylori: 20mg twice daily with antibiotics for 7-14 days. Maintenance: 20mg once daily.
drugs.se: Common: headache, nausea, diarrhea, constipation. Long-term: low magnesium, vitamin B12 deficiency, increased fracture risk, kidney disease risk.
drugs.w: Take 30-60 minutes before eating. Do not use long-term without regular medical review. May mask symptoms of stomach cancer. Do not crush or chew delayed-release capsules.

drugs.u: Sildenafil is a PDE5 inhibitor that enhances the natural erectile response to sexual stimulation by increasing blood flow to the penis. It requires sexual stimulation to work. At lower doses it is used for pulmonary arterial hypertension.
drugs.s: Erectile dysfunction (impotence), Pulmonary arterial hypertension (Revatio at lower doses), Raynaud's phenomenon (off-label)
drugs.d: Erectile dysfunction: 25-100mg taken 30-60 minutes before sexual activity. Start with 50mg. Maximum once per day. Effect lasts 4-6 hours. Pulmonary hypertension: 20mg three times daily.
drugs.se: Common: headache (most common), facial flushing, dyspepsia, nasal congestion, dizziness, visual disturbances. Serious: priapism (prolonged erection), sudden vision loss, sudden hearing loss, severe hypotension.
drugs.w: NEVER take with nitrates as this causes potentially fatal severe hypotension. Avoid with alpha-blockers unless on stable dose. Priapism lasting more than 4 hours is a medical emergency.

drugs.u: Montelukast is a leukotriene receptor antagonist that reduces bronchoconstriction, mucus secretion, and airway inflammation. Particularly useful for patients with both asthma and allergic rhinitis as it helps both conditions. Once daily dosing in the evening.
drugs.s: Chronic asthma prevention (not acute attacks), Seasonal and perennial allergic rhinitis, Exercise-induced bronchoconstriction, Asthma with allergies, Chronic urticaria (hives)
drugs.d: Adults and adolescents over 15: 10mg once daily in the evening. Children 6-14 years: 5mg chewable tablet once daily. Children 2-5 years: 4mg granules once daily.
drugs.se: Generally well tolerated. Common: headache, abdominal pain, diarrhea, dizziness. Rare but important: neuropsychiatric effects including agitation, depression, sleep disturbances, suicidal thinking.
drugs.w: Report any neuropsychiatric symptoms immediately. Not for acute asthma attacks. Continue other asthma medicines unless advised otherwise. Black box warning added for neuropsychiatric events.

drugs.u: Diazepam is a benzodiazepine that enhances GABA activity in the brain causing sedative, anxiolytic, anticonvulsant, and muscle relaxant effects. Despite its effectiveness it carries significant risks of tolerance, dependence, and addiction, limiting use to short-term treatment.
drugs.s: Anxiety disorders (short-term only), Acute muscle spasm, Alcohol withdrawal syndrome, Status epilepticus (IV), Pre-procedural anxiolysis, Spasticity from neurological conditions
drugs.d: Anxiety: 2-10mg two to four times daily. Muscle spasm: 5-15mg daily in divided doses. Alcohol withdrawal: 10-20mg initially then reduced. Maximum 2-4 weeks for anxiety.
drugs.se: Common: drowsiness, sedation, poor coordination, dizziness, confusion, memory impairment, weakness. Paradoxical reactions in some patients.
drugs.w: Highly addictive. Maximum 2-4 weeks use. Do not stop suddenly after prolonged use (seizures possible). Do not drive. Fatal interaction with alcohol and other CNS depressants.

drugs.u: Carbamazepine stabilizes neuronal membranes by blocking sodium channels. Very effective for partial seizures and trigeminal neuralgia. It induces its own metabolism and many other drug interactions. Genetic testing recommended before use in Asian patients.
drugs.s: Epilepsy (partial and tonic-clonic seizures), Trigeminal neuralgia (severe facial nerve pain - very effective), Bipolar disorder, Neuropathic pain, Alcohol withdrawal seizures
drugs.d: Epilepsy adults: Start 100-200mg once or twice daily. Increase by 200mg weekly to 800-1200mg daily in divided doses. Trigeminal neuralgia: 200-400mg initially increasing to 400-800mg daily.
drugs.se: Common when starting: drowsiness, dizziness, double vision, nausea, unsteadiness. Serious: severe skin reactions (especially in Asian patients), bone marrow suppression, liver toxicity, low sodium.
drugs.w: Genetic testing for HLA-B*1502 allele recommended in patients of Asian ancestry including Pakistani patients. Regular blood counts and liver tests. Never stop suddenly. Many drug interactions.

drugs.u: Glimepiride is a third-generation sulfonylurea with lower hypoglycemia risk than older sulfonylureas and once-daily dosing. It stimulates insulin secretion and is often combined with metformin for improved blood glucose control.
drugs.s: Type 2 diabetes mellitus, Elevated blood glucose not controlled by diet and exercise alone, Type 2 diabetes in combination with metformin or insulin
drugs.d: Adults: Start 1-2mg once daily with first main meal. Increase by 1mg every 1-2 weeks. Usual dose 1-4mg daily. Maximum 6-8mg daily. Elderly: start with 1mg.
drugs.se: Most important: hypoglycemia with shakiness, sweating, palpitations, confusion. Weight gain. Less common: nausea, stomach upset, skin rash.
drugs.w: Risk of hypoglycemia especially if meals are skipped. Patients and families must know hypoglycemia symptoms and treatment. Monitor blood glucose regularly. Use caution in elderly and kidney or liver disease.

drugs.u: Methotrexate is used at low weekly doses for autoimmune diseases and at high doses for certain cancers. Cornerstone treatment for rheumatoid arthritis and psoriasis. Folic acid supplementation is essential to reduce side effects.
drugs.s: Rheumatoid arthritis (gold standard DMARD), Psoriasis moderate to severe, Psoriatic arthritis, Inflammatory bowel disease, Systemic lupus erythematosus, Certain cancers at high doses, Juvenile idiopathic arthritis
drugs.d: Rheumatoid arthritis and psoriasis: 7.5-25mg ONCE WEEKLY. Never daily as this causes fatal toxicity. Start 7.5mg weekly. Folic acid 5mg weekly on a different day must be taken.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, fatigue, mouth sores, hair thinning, elevated liver enzymes. Serious: liver fibrosis, lung inflammation, severe bone marrow suppression, serious infections. Highly teratogenic.
drugs.w: MUST be taken ONCE WEEKLY not daily. Fatal errors have occurred from daily dosing. Take folic acid on different days. Completely avoid alcohol. Highly teratogenic. Effective contraception essential. Regular blood tests essential.

drugs.u: Tramadol is a centrally acting analgesic with dual mechanisms: opioid receptor binding and inhibition of serotonin and noradrenaline reuptake. Carries significant risks of addiction, dependence, and overdose. Used for moderate to severe pain not responding to non-opioid analgesics.
drugs.s: Moderate to severe acute pain, Post-operative pain, Injury and trauma pain, Back pain, Cancer pain, Neuropathic pain, Arthritis pain not responding to simpler medicines, Dental pain
drugs.d: Adults: 50-100mg every 4-6 hours as needed. Maximum 400mg per day. Elderly: start with lower doses. Modified-release: 100-300mg once or twice daily. Do not crush modified-release tablets.
drugs.se: Common: nausea (very common), vomiting, constipation, dizziness, headache, drowsiness, dry mouth. Serious: seizures, serotonin syndrome, respiratory depression, dependence and addiction.
drugs.w: High risk of addiction and dependence. Do not exceed dose. Never combine with alcohol or benzodiazepines (can be fatal). Do not stop suddenly after prolonged use.

drugs.u: Spironolactone is a potassium-sparing diuretic and aldosterone antagonist. In heart failure it reduces mortality at low doses. It also has antiandrogenic properties useful for PCOS, female androgenic alopecia, and acne in women.
drugs.s: Heart failure (proven mortality benefit), Fluid retention from liver cirrhosis, Primary hyperaldosteronism, Resistant hypertension, Edema, PCOS (hormonal effects), Female androgenic alopecia, Hormonal acne in women
drugs.d: Heart failure: 25-50mg once daily. Ascites: 100-400mg daily. Hypertension: 25-100mg daily. Anti-androgenic PCOS or acne: 50-200mg daily. Take with food.
drugs.se: Common: increased urination, breast tenderness and enlargement in men (gynecomastia), menstrual irregularities, decreased libido in men, dizziness, high potassium, nausea.
drugs.w: Monitor potassium and kidney function closely. Dangerous hyperkalemia possible especially with ACE inhibitors, ARBs, or potassium supplements. Can cause gynecomastia in men.

drugs.u: Levofloxacin is a respiratory fluoroquinolone with excellent activity against respiratory pathogens including Streptococcus pneumoniae. Particularly valuable for community-acquired pneumonia and serious respiratory infections. Called a respiratory quinolone due to enhanced respiratory organism activity.
drugs.s: Community-acquired pneumonia including drug-resistant pneumococcal, Nosocomial pneumonia, Acute bacterial exacerbation of chronic bronchitis, Acute bacterial sinusitis, Urinary tract infections including pyelonephritis, Skin and soft tissue infections, Anthrax
drugs.d: Adults: 250-750mg once daily depending on infection type. CAP: 500-750mg once daily for 7-14 days. UTI: 250mg once daily for 3-10 days. Take at same time each day.
drugs.se: Similar to ciprofloxacin: nausea, diarrhea, headache, dizziness, insomnia. Serious: tendon rupture, peripheral neuropathy, CNS effects, heart rhythm problems, hypoglycemia in diabetics.
drugs.w: Black box warning: tendon rupture and permanent peripheral neuropathy. Stop immediately if tendon pain occurs. Avoid in children. Reserve for serious infections where no alternatives exist.

drugs.u: Naproxen is an NSAID providing longer lasting pain relief than ibuprofen allowing twice-daily dosing rather than three or four times daily. Effective for arthritis, menstrual cramps, tendinitis, bursitis, and gout. More convenient than shorter-acting NSAIDs.
drugs.s: Osteoarthritis, Rheumatoid arthritis, Ankylosing spondylitis, Menstrual cramps, Gout attacks, Tendinitis, Bursitis, Headache, Dental pain, Musculoskeletal pain and inflammation
drugs.d: Adults: 250-500mg twice daily with food. Maximum 1250mg on day 1 then 1000mg daily. For gout: 750mg initially then 250mg every 8 hours.
drugs.se: Common: stomach upset, heartburn, nausea, headache. Serious: stomach ulcers, kidney damage, increased blood pressure, cardiovascular risk.
drugs.w: Take with food. Avoid with other NSAIDs. Use lowest dose for shortest time. Avoid in pregnancy. Monitor kidney function.

drugs.u: Clarithromycin is a macrolide antibiotic with excellent activity against typical and atypical respiratory pathogens. Key component of H. pylori eradication regimens. Extended-release allows once-daily dosing improving compliance.
drugs.s: Community-acquired pneumonia, Upper respiratory tract infections, Acute bronchitis exacerbation, H. pylori eradication (with PPI and another antibiotic), Skin and soft tissue infections, MAC in HIV, Lyme disease
drugs.d: Adults: 250-500mg twice daily for 7-14 days. Extended-release: 500-1000mg once daily. H. pylori: 500mg twice daily with amoxicillin and omeprazole for 7-14 days. Take with food.
drugs.se: Common: nausea, diarrhea, abnormal taste (metallic or bitter), abdominal pain, headache. Serious: liver toxicity, QT interval prolongation, C. difficile colitis, drug interactions.
drugs.w: Many important drug interactions as strong inhibitor of CYP3A4. Tell doctor all medicines being taken. Avoid in pregnancy especially first trimester.
